String.description: A UTF-8–encoded, growable string. The most common string type in Rust, owning its contents.

push.signature: pub fn push(&mut self, ch: char)
push.description: Appends the given `char` to the end of this `String`.

push_str.description: Appends a given string slice onto the end of this `String`.

len.description: Returns the length of this `String`, in bytes, not `char`s or graphemes.

is_empty.description: Returns `true` if this `String` has a length of zero.

contains.signature: pub fn contains<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>(&'a self, pat: P) -> bool
contains.description: Returns `true` if the given pattern matches a sub-slice of this string slice.

replace.signature: pub fn replace<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>(&'a self, from: P, to: &str) -> String
replace.description: Replaces all matches of a pattern with another string.

trim.description: Returns a string slice with leading and trailing whitespace removed.

to_uppercase.description: Returns the uppercase equivalent of this string slice, as a new `String`.

to_lowercase.description: Returns the lowercase equivalent of this string slice, as a new `String`.

as_str.description: Extracts a string slice containing the entire `String`.

as_bytes.description: Returns a byte slice of this `String`'s contents.

chars.description: Returns an iterator over the `char`s of this string slice.

split.signature: pub fn split<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>(&'a self, pat: P) -> Split<'a, P>
split.description: Returns an iterator over substrings of this string slice, separated by a pattern.

starts_with.signature: pub fn starts_with<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>(&'a self, pat: P) -> bool
starts_with.description: Returns `true` if the given pattern matches a prefix of this string slice.

ends_with.signature: pub fn ends_with<'a, P: Pattern<'a>>(&'a self, pat: P) -> bool
ends_with.description: Returns `true` if the given pattern matches a suffix of this string slice.

into_bytes.description: Converts this `String` into a byte vector, consuming the string.

Vec.description: A contiguous growable array type. Provides O(1) indexed access, amortized O(1) push to the end, and O(n) insert and removal.

new.description: Constructs a new, empty `Vec<T>`. Does not allocate until elements are pushed.

with_capacity.description: Constructs a new, empty `Vec<T>` with at least the specified capacity.

push.signature: pub fn push(&mut self, value: T)
push.description: Appends an element to the back of the collection.

pop.description: Removes the last element from the vector and returns it, or `None` if empty.

len.description: Returns the number of elements in the vector.

is_empty.description: Returns `true` if the vector contains no elements.

contains.signature: pub fn contains(&self, x: &T) -> bool where T: PartialEq
contains.description: Returns `true` if the slice contains an element with the given value.

iter.description: Returns an iterator over the slice.

iter_mut.signature: pub fn iter_mut(&mut self) -> IterMut<'_, T>
iter_mut.description: Returns a mutable iterator over the slice.

sort.signature: pub fn sort(&mut self) where T: Ord
sort.description: Sorts the slice in ascending order. This sort is stable (preserves order of equal elements).

sort_by.signature: pub fn sort_by<F>(&mut self, compare: F) where F: FnMut(&T, &T) -> Ordering
sort_by.description: Sorts the slice with a comparator function. Stable sort.

retain.signature: pub fn retain<F>(&mut self, f: F) where F: FnMut(&T) -> bool
retain.description: Retains only the elements specified by the predicate.

extend.signature: pub fn extend<I: IntoIterator<Item = T>>(&mut self, iter: I)
extend.description: Extends the collection with the contents of an iterator.

drain.signature: pub fn drain<R: RangeBounds<usize>>(&mut self, range: R) -> Drain<'_, T>
drain.description: Removes the specified range from the vector in bulk, returning an iterator over the removed items.

truncate.signature: pub fn truncate(&mut self, len: usize)
truncate.description: Shortens the vector, keeping the first `len` elements and dropping the rest.

clear.description: Clears the vector, removing all values. Does not affect allocated capacity.

get.signature: pub fn get<I: SliceIndex<[T]>>(&self, index: I) -> Option<&I::Output>
get.description: Returns a reference to an element or subslice, or `None` if out of bounds.

first.description: Returns the first element of the slice, or `None` if empty.

last.description: Returns the last element of the slice, or `None` if empty.

remove.signature: pub fn remove(&mut self, index: usize) -> T
remove.description: Removes and returns the element at position `index`, shifting all elements after it to the left.

insert.signature: pub fn insert(&mut self, index: usize, element: T)
insert.description: Inserts an element at position `index`, shifting all elements after it to the right.

swap_remove.signature: pub fn swap_remove(&mut self, index: usize) -> T
swap_remove.description: Removes an element from the vector and returns it, replacing it with the last element. O(1) but does not preserve ordering.

dedup.signature: pub fn dedup(&mut self) where T: PartialEq
dedup.description: Removes consecutive repeated elements. If the vector is sorted, this removes all duplicates.

binary_search.signature: pub fn binary_search(&self, x: &T) -> Result<usize, usize> where T: Ord
binary_search.description: Binary searches for a given element. If found, returns `Ok(index)`. If not found, returns `Err(index)` where the element could be inserted.

windows.signature: pub fn windows(&self, size: usize) -> Windows<'_, T>
windows.description: Returns an iterator over overlapping sub-slices of the given size.

chunks.signature: pub fn chunks(&self, chunk_size: usize) -> Chunks<'_, T>
chunks.description: Returns an iterator over `chunk_size` elements of the slice at a time.

HashMap.signature: pub struct HashMap<K, V, S = RandomState>
HashMap.description: A hash map implemented with quadratic probing and SIMD lookup. Requires keys to implement Eq and Hash.

insert.signature: pub fn insert(&mut self, k: K, v: V) -> Option<V>
insert.description: Inserts a key-value pair. If the key existed, the old value is returned.

get.signature: pub fn get<Q: ?Sized>(&self, k: &Q) -> Option<&V> where K: Borrow<Q>, Q: Hash + Eq
get.description: Returns a reference to the value corresponding to the key.

get_mut.signature: pub fn get_mut<Q: ?Sized>(&mut self, k: &Q) -> Option<&mut V> where K: Borrow<Q>, Q: Hash + Eq
get_mut.description: Returns a mutable reference to the value corresponding to the key.

remove.signature: pub fn remove<Q: ?Sized>(&mut self, k: &Q) -> Option<V> where K: Borrow<Q>, Q: Hash + Eq
remove.description: Removes a key from the map, returning the value if the key was present.

contains_key.signature: pub fn contains_key<Q: ?Sized>(&self, k: &Q) -> bool where K: Borrow<Q>, Q: Hash + Eq
contains_key.description: Returns `true` if the map contains a value for the specified key.

keys.signature: pub fn keys(&self) -> Keys<'_, K, V>
keys.description: Returns an iterator visiting all keys in arbitrary order.

values.signature: pub fn values(&self) -> Values<'_, K, V>
values.description: Returns an iterator visiting all values in arbitrary order.

iter.signature: pub fn iter(&self) -> Iter<'_, K, V>
iter.description: Returns an iterator visiting all key-value pairs in arbitrary order.

len.description: Returns the number of elements in the map.

is_empty.description: Returns `true` if the map contains no elements.

entry.signature: pub fn entry(&mut self, key: K) -> Entry<'_, K, V>
entry.description: Gets the given key's corresponding entry in the map for in-place manipulation.

extend.signature: pub fn extend<I: IntoIterator<Item = (K, V)>>(&mut self, iter: I)
extend.description: Extends the map with the contents of an iterator of key-value pairs.

drain.signature: pub fn drain(&mut self) -> Drain<'_, K, V>
drain.description: Clears the map, returning all key-value pairs as an iterator.

retain.signature: pub fn retain<F>(&mut self, f: F) where F: FnMut(&K, &mut V) -> bool
retain.description: Retains only the elements specified by the predicate.

Option.description: Represents an optional value. Every Option is either Some containing a value, or None.

unwrap.description: Returns the contained `Some` value. Panics if the value is `None`.

unwrap_or.signature: pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T
unwrap_or.description: Returns the contained `Some` value or a provided default.

unwrap_or_else.signature: pub fn unwrap_or_else<F: FnOnce() -> T>(self, f: F) -> T
unwrap_or_else.description: Returns the contained `Some` value or computes it from a closure.

unwrap_or_default.signature: pub fn unwrap_or_default(self) -> T where T: Default
unwrap_or_default.description: Returns the contained `Some` value or the default value of `T`.

map.signature: pub fn map<U, F: FnOnce(T) -> U>(self, f: F) -> Option<U>
map.description: Maps an `Option<T>` to `Option<U>` by applying a function to the contained value.

and_then.signature: pub fn and_then<U, F: FnOnce(T) -> Option<U>>(self, f: F) -> Option<U>
and_then.description: Returns `None` if the option is `None`, otherwise calls `f` with the wrapped value and returns the result. Also known as flat_map.

or.signature: pub fn or(self, optb: Option<T>) -> Option<T>
or.description: Returns the option if it contains a value, otherwise returns `optb`.

or_else.signature: pub fn or_else<F: FnOnce() -> Option<T>>(self, f: F) -> Option<T>
or_else.description: Returns the option if it contains a value, otherwise calls `f` and returns the result.

filter.signature: pub fn filter<P: FnOnce(&T) -> bool>(self, predicate: P) -> Option<T>
filter.description: Returns `None` if the option is `None`, otherwise calls `predicate` and returns `Some(t)` if the predicate returns `true`.

is_some.description: Returns `true` if the option is a `Some` value.

is_none.description: Returns `true` if the option is a `None` value.

ok_or.signature: pub fn ok_or<E>(self, err: E) -> Result<T, E>
ok_or.description: Transforms the `Option<T>` into a `Result<T, E>`, mapping `Some(v)` to `Ok(v)` and `None` to `Err(err)`.

flatten.signature: pub fn flatten(self) -> Option<T> where T: Option<T>

zip.signature: pub fn zip<U>(self, other: Option<U>) -> Option<(T, U)>
zip.description: Zips `self` with another `Option`. Returns `Some((a, b))` if both are `Some`, otherwise `None`.

take.description: Takes the value out of the option, leaving a `None` in its place.

Result.signature: pub enum Result<T, E> { Ok(T), Err(E) }
Result.description: A type used for returning and propagating errors. Ok(T) represents success, Err(E) represents failure.

unwrap.signature: pub fn unwrap(self) -> T where E: Debug
unwrap.description: Returns the contained `Ok` value. Panics if the value is an `Err`, with a panic message from the error's `Debug` impl.

unwrap_or.signature: pub fn unwrap_or(self, default: T) -> T
unwrap_or.description: Returns the contained `Ok` value or a provided default.

unwrap_err.signature: pub fn unwrap_err(self) -> E where T: Debug
unwrap_err.description: Returns the contained `Err` value. Panics if the value is `Ok`.

map.signature: pub fn map<U, F: FnOnce(T) -> U>(self, op: F) -> Result<U, E>
map.description: Maps a `Result<T, E>` to `Result<U, E>` by applying a function to a contained `Ok` value.

map_err.signature: pub fn map_err<F, O: FnOnce(E) -> F>(self, op: O) -> Result<T, F>
map_err.description: Maps a `Result<T, E>` to `Result<T, F>` by applying a function to a contained `Err` value.

and_then.signature: pub fn and_then<U, F: FnOnce(T) -> Result<U, E>>(self, op: F) -> Result<U, E>
and_then.description: Calls `op` if the result is `Ok`, otherwise returns the `Err` value of self.

or.signature: pub fn or<F>(self, res: Result<T, F>) -> Result<T, F>
or.description: Returns `res` if the result is `Err`, otherwise returns the `Ok` value of self.

or_else.signature: pub fn or_else<F, O: FnOnce(E) -> Result<T, F>>(self, op: O) -> Result<T, F>
or_else.description: Calls `op` if the result is `Err`, otherwise returns the `Ok` value of self.

ok.description: Converts from `Result<T, E>` to `Option<T>`, discarding the error.

err.description: Converts from `Result<T, E>` to `Option<E>`, discarding the success value.

expect.signature: pub fn expect(self, msg: &str) -> T where E: Debug
expect.description: Returns the contained `Ok` value. Panics with the provided message if `Err`.

Box.description: A pointer type for heap allocation. Box<T> provides ownership of a heap-allocated value of type T.

new.description: Allocates memory on the heap and places `x` into it.

into_raw.description: Consumes the `Box`, returning a raw pointer. The caller is responsible for freeing the memory.

from_raw.signature: pub unsafe fn from_raw(raw: *mut T) -> Box<T>
from_raw.description: Constructs a `Box<T>` from a raw pointer. Undefined behavior if the pointer was not allocated by `Box`.

leak.signature: pub fn leak<'a>(b: Box<T>) -> &'a mut T
leak.description: Consumes and leaks the `Box`, returning a mutable reference with a `'static` lifetime.

Rc.description: A single-threaded reference-counting pointer. Rc provides shared ownership of a value of type T, allocated on the heap.

clone.description: Creates a new pointer to the same allocation, incrementing the reference count.

strong_count.description: Gets the number of strong (`Rc`) pointers to this allocation.

try_unwrap.signature: pub fn try_unwrap(this: Rc<T>) -> Result<T, Rc<T>>
try_unwrap.description: Returns the inner value if the `Rc` has exactly one strong reference.

downgrade.description: Creates a new `Weak` pointer to this allocation.

Arc.description: A thread-safe reference-counting pointer. Arc stands for Atomically Reference Counted.

clone.description: Creates a new pointer to the same allocation, atomically incrementing the reference count.

strong_count.description: Gets the number of strong (`Arc`) pointers to this allocation.

try_unwrap.signature: pub fn try_unwrap(this: Arc<T>) -> Result<T, Arc<T>>
try_unwrap.description: Returns the inner value if the `Arc` has exactly one strong reference.

downgrade.description: Creates a new `Weak` pointer to this allocation.

Iterator.description: A trait for dealing with iterators. Only the next method is required; all other methods have default implementations.

next.description: Advances the iterator and returns the next value. Returns `None` when iteration is complete.

map.signature: fn map<B, F: FnMut(Self::Item) -> B>(self, f: F) -> Map<Self, F>
map.description: Takes a closure and creates an iterator that calls that closure on each element.

filter.signature: fn filter<P: FnMut(&Self::Item) -> bool>(self, predicate: P) -> Filter<Self, P>
filter.description: Creates an iterator that only yields elements matching the predicate.

fold.signature: fn fold<B, F: FnMut(B, Self::Item) -> B>(self, init: B, f: F) -> B
fold.description: Folds every element into an accumulator by applying a closure, returning the final result.

collect.description: Transforms an iterator into a collection. Can collect into Vec, HashMap, String, etc.

enumerate.description: Creates an iterator that yields `(index, value)` pairs.

zip.signature: fn zip<U: IntoIterator>(self, other: U) -> Zip<Self, U::IntoIter>
zip.description: Zips two iterators into a single iterator of pairs.

take.description: Creates an iterator that yields at most `n` elements.

skip.description: Creates an iterator that skips the first `n` elements.

chain.signature: fn chain<U: IntoIterator<Item = Self::Item>>(self, other: U) -> Chain<Self, U::IntoIter>
chain.description: Creates an iterator that chains two iterators together, yielding elements from the first then the second.

flatten.signature: fn flatten(self) -> Flatten<Self> where Self::Item: IntoIterator
flatten.description: Creates an iterator that flattens nested structure.

any.signature: fn any<F: FnMut(Self::Item) -> bool>(&mut self, f: F) -> bool
any.description: Tests if any element of the iterator matches a predicate.

all.signature: fn all<F: FnMut(Self::Item) -> bool>(&mut self, f: F) -> bool
all.description: Tests if every element of the iterator matches a predicate.

find.signature: fn find<P: FnMut(&Self::Item) -> bool>(&mut self, predicate: P) -> Option<Self::Item>
find.description: Searches for an element that satisfies the predicate.

position.signature: fn position<P: FnMut(Self::Item) -> bool>(&mut self, predicate: P) -> Option<usize>
position.description: Searches for an element, returning its index.

count.description: Consumes the iterator, counting the number of elements.

sum.description: Sums the elements of an iterator.

product.description: Iterates over the entire iterator, multiplying all elements.

min.signature: fn min(self) -> Option<Self::Item> where Self::Item: Ord
min.description: Returns the minimum element of an iterator.

max.signature: fn max(self) -> Option<Self::Item> where Self::Item: Ord
max.description: Returns the maximum element of an iterator.

cloned.signature: fn cloned<'a, T: 'a + Clone>(self) -> Cloned<Self> where Self: Iterator<Item = &'a T>
cloned.description: Creates an iterator that clones all of its elements.

copied.signature: fn copied<'a, T: 'a + Copy>(self) -> Copied<Self> where Self: Iterator<Item = &'a T>
copied.description: Creates an iterator that copies all of its elements.

peekable.description: Creates an iterator that can use the `peek` method to look at the next element without consuming it.

rev.signature: fn rev(self) -> Rev<Self> where Self: DoubleEndedIterator
